question: Which deep dive covers barge-in, and what is barge-in?
keypoints: barge-in is handling the user interrupting mid-response

question: Which deep dive has me write the agent loop from scratch?
Which deep dive covers the Model Context Protocol?
keypoints: write the server once, any client can use it

question: Which deep dive teaches defending against prompt injection?
Which deep dive runs entirely on an offline mock provider, needing no API key at all?
Which deep dive should I do to run open-weight models on my own machine?
Which deep dive teaches changing a model's behavior by training on examples?
Which deep dive covers hooks, permission policies, sandboxing, and subagents?
Which deep dive covers conversation memory and compaction?
Which deep dive covers putting images and audio into prompts?
Which deep dive is about making app quality a number you can rerun?
Which deep dive teaches chunking, embeddings, and vector search?
What model does the series default to, and why?
keypoints: cheap and fast, good for learning

question: Where do my API keys go in this series, and where do they explicitly NOT go?
keypoints: OS keychain, injected per-command with secrun

question: What are the two knobs that matter when chunking documents, and what tradeoff does each control?
keypoints: chunk size: too big is unfocused, too small lacks enough to answer
overlap: boundary sentences aren't lost, costs duplication

question: According to the RAG dive, what is a vector store at heart?
Which embedding provider does the Claude stack use, and why not Anthropic's own?
keypoints: Anthropic has no first-party embeddings model

question: What three questions does BM25 ask about each query word?
keypoints: term frequency (how often in the chunk)
inverse document frequency (how rare across the corpus)

question: What is the order of the eight core deep dives?
keypoints: OpenAI API, Claude API, Prompt Engineering, RAG, Evals, Agents, Prompt Injection & Guardrails, Production

question: What did the series learn about hybrid retrieval being 'strictly better' than vector search?
keypoints: a 50/50 blend ranked the right chunk worse than vector-only on a paraphrase query

question: How do I run a lesson script that needs an API key?
What should I run first in every repo of the series, and what does it do?
keypoints: checks Python version, packages, provider, key; makes no API call

question: What three verdicts can the harness permission policy return, and what does each mean?
Why does the RAG dive say splitting markdown on headings beats a fixed-size window?
keypoints: each section is about one thing
fixed windows glue the tail of one topic onto the head of the next

question: In the RAG dive's store, what does cosine_similarity return when one vector is all zeros?
What are the two BM25 knobs in the RAG dive's keyword index, and their default values?
What does secrun do when a required Keychain item is missing?
keypoints: prints how to store it with security add-generic-password

question: What is the input_type hint on the RAG dive's embed() helper, and which provider actually uses it?
keypoints: Voyage uses it; OpenAI ignores it

question: In the agents dive, when does the agent loop stop?
keypoints: when the model responds with no tool calls

question: What does the RAG dive's store.py say you switch to when you have millions of vectors?
keypoints: approximate-nearest-neighbour index (FAISS, hnswlib) or a hosted vector database

question: How do the evals and fine-tuning dives relate to each other?
keypoints: fine-tuning must be proven against a baseline
evals provide the measurement; fine-tuning slots in after RAG and Evals

question: Which bonus dives does the series say pair with RAG?
What is the through-line thread of the eight core dives, in the series' own words?
keypoints: build the call, ask well, ground it, measure it, let it act, harden it, operate it

question: What is the one big idea of the prompt injection dive, and what scope does the series put on its attacks?
keypoints: treat everything the model reads and writes as untrusted; contain the blast radius
strictly defensive: every attack targets only its own toy bot with a made-up passphrase

question: What can I learn in this series for $0?
keypoints: the Production dive on its offline mock
the offline first sections of most repos
